description:
## Component: hx-breadcrumb (NEW)
**Tier 1 — Universal Foundation | Industry Consensus: 4/6 libraries**

Hierarchical page path navigation showing current location in site structure.

### ADR-001 Classification: Slot-Driven (Composite)
Drupal controls breadcrumb items via slots. Component provides separator styling and ARIA navigation landmark.

### API Design
**Properties:** `separator` (string, default "/"), `label` (aria-label for nav, default "Breadcrumb")
**Slots:** Default (hx-breadcrumb-item or `<a>` children)
**CSS Parts:** `nav`, `list`, `separator`

### Child: hx-breadcrumb-item
**Properties:** `href` (string — omit for current page/last item)
**Slots:** Default (link text)
**CSS Parts:** `link`, `text`

### Drupal Integration
```twig
{# Drupal breadcrumb preprocess renders items #}
<hx-breadcrumb>
  {% for item in breadcrumb %}
    <hx-breadcrumb-item {% if item.url %}href="{{ item.url }}"{% endif %}>
      {{ item.text }}
    </hx-breadcrumb-item>
  {% endfor %}
</hx-breadcrumb>
```

Drupal's breadcrumb system (hook_system_breadcrumb_alter, BreadcrumbBuilder) controls the items. Component provides consistent visual treatment and ARIA navigation landmark.

### Healthcare: Deep navigation in clinical portals (Home > Department > Patient > Record > Lab Results)

### Acceptance Criteria
1. Last item renders as text (no link) — current page
2. Separator between items (customizable)
3. WCAG 2.1 AA: nav landmark with aria-label, aria-current="page" on last
4. Responsive: collapses middle items on small screens with ellipsis
5. Works with Drupal's breadcrumb system
title: T1-18: hx-breadcrumb — Hierarchical Navigation

coderabbitai: **Actionable comments posted: 3**

<details>

Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

Inline comments:
In
`@packages/hx-library/src/components/hx-breadcrumb/hx-breadcrumb-item.styles.ts`:
- Around line 10-11: Remove the hardcoded fallback literals from all CSS custom
property usages in hx-breadcrumb-item.styles.ts so the styles rely only on
design tokens; specifically replace multi-level var(...) expressions like color:
var(--hx-breadcrumb-link-color, var(--hx-color-primary-600, `#2563eb`)); and
similar declarations for padding, gap, and border-radius with single-token
references (e.g., color: var(--hx-breadcrumb-link-color); padding:
var(--hx-breadcrumb-item-padding); gap: var(--hx-breadcrumb-gap); border-radius:
var(--hx-breadcrumb-border-radius);) and update the same change for the other
occurrences called out in the review (the anchor/link rule, chip/pill styles,
and spacing/gap rules) so no hex or rem fallback values remain.

In `@packages/hx-library/src/components/hx-breadcrumb/hx-breadcrumb.stories.ts`:
- Around line 10-47: The Storybook types are unparameterized causing weak
`args`; define a props type (e.g., interface BreadcrumbProps { separator?:
string; label?: string }) that matches the component argTypes, import the
Storybook generic types (Meta, StoryObj) if not already, then parameterize the
meta and story types by replacing the unparameterized uses with
Meta<BreadcrumbProps> (or use "satisfies Meta<BreadcrumbProps>") and type Story
= StoryObj<BreadcrumbProps>; update any downstream story vars to use that Story
type so `args` are strongly typed and no `any` is emitted.

In `@packages/hx-library/src/components/hx-breadcrumb/hx-breadcrumb.styles.ts`:
- Around line 6-7: Remove the hardcoded fallbacks in hx-breadcrumb styles:
replace the literal "sans-serif" and "0.875rem" fallbacks with only design
tokens so typography is fully tokenized

threadFeedback.agentReasoning: hx-breadcrumb-item.styles.ts still has hardcoded hex fallbacks (#2563eb, #1d4ed8, #3b82f6, #374151, #9ca3af, 0.125rem, 0.25rem) as third-level fallbacks. The project's zero-tolerance policy explicitly prohibits hardcoded values — colors and spacing must use design tokens exclusively. Two-level token chains are the correct pattern.

hx-breadcrumb.styles.ts line 7 still has `var(--hx-font-size-sm, 0.875rem)` — a hardcoded rem value violating the same zero-tolerance policy. Also `sans-serif` on line 6 is a hardcoded font-family fallback that should be dropped in favor of a two-level token chain.
